title: Animating juggling patterns
text: Pass pattern string:  Positron RStudio, animation appears Viewer pane. Otherwise opens browser. can also pass Siteswap object directly — jugglr extracts sequence string : animation identical either way. One limitation: passingSiteswap objects using fractional notation (e.g. "<4.5 3 3 | 3 4 3.5>") can’t animated JugglingLab doesn’t recognise format. P-notation passing patterns (e.g. "<3p 3|3p 3>") work fine.
code: animate("531") animate(siteswap("531"))

title: Animating juggling patterns
text: colors argument visually impactful option. colours specified — JugglingLab uses default (single colour): colors = "mixed" — prop gets different colour. Useful tracking individual balls pattern:  colors = "orbits" — props follow path pattern share colour. analytically useful mode: shows orbit structure glance:  Custom colours — vector R colour names hex codes, one per prop:  Okabe-Ito colours used timeline() ladder() — good choice want consistency across visualisations. Note specifying colours takes noticeably longer default, coloured version isn’t typically cached JugglingLab server.
code: animate("531") animate("531", colors = "mixed") animate("531", colors = "orbits") animate("531", colors = c("#E69F00", "#56B4E9", "#009E73"))

title: Animating juggling patterns
text: default prop ball. Rings image prop also available:
code: animate("531", prop = "ring") animate("531", prop = "image")

title: Animating juggling patterns
text: Two parameters control animation speed. slowdown stretches throw arcs time — JugglingLab default 2.0. Increase slow animation , useful ’re studying new pattern:  bps sets beats per second — tempo pattern. Increase see pattern full juggling speed:  width height control pixel dimensions animation need specific size.
code: animate("531", slowdown = 4) animate("531", bps = 8)

title: Animating juggling patterns
text: embed animation R Markdown Quarto document, save disk first reference file: separate chunk: Set display options via chunk arguments: .width = "40%" keeps GIF filling full page width. approach used throughout vignette.
code: animate("531", path = "figures/531.gif", colors = "orbits") knitr::include_graphics("figures/531.gif")

title: Animating juggling patterns
text: animate() passes additional named arguments JugglingLab. worth knowing: gravity: default feels like Earth. Set gravity = 700 pattern looks like ’s juggled somewhere lower gravity:  propdiam: prop diameter metres. Adjust props look large small relative juggler. camangle: camera angle degrees around vertical axis. Useful different perspective pattern. showground: set true show ground plane. full list parameters documented JugglingLab GIF server reference.
code: animate("531", gravity = 700)

title: Get started with jugglr
text: simplest form vanilla siteswap: one prop thrown per beat, hands alternating. Create pattern siteswap(): print method tells four things: whether pattern valid, many props requires, period (many beats repeats), whether ’s symmetrical — meaning hands thing, just offset time. ’s trickier pattern: Still three props, period 3. 4 high self-throw buys moment, 2 low hold, 3 standard cascade throw. pattern starts take shape even numbers. jugglers working towards five balls:
code: siteswap("3") #> ✔ '3' is valid vanilla siteswap #> ℹ It uses 3 props #> ℹ It is symmetrical with period 1 ss_423 <- siteswap("423") ss_423 #> ✔ '423' is valid vanilla siteswap #> ℹ It uses 3 props #> ℹ It is symmetrical with period 3 siteswap("5") #> ✔ '5' is valid vanilla siteswap #> ℹ It uses 5 props #> ℹ It is symmetrical with period 1

title: Get started with jugglr
text: every sequence numbers makes jugglable pattern. jugglr checks validity construction: Two problems, reported separately. average 2 1 1.5, require 1.5 props — impossible. even setting aside, two throws land beat: collision. diagrams visualising patterns section make concrete. genuinely useful ’re inventing patterns. Check sequence picking clubs.
code: ss_21 <- siteswap("21") ss_21 #> ✖ '21' is not a valid juggling pattern #> ℹ The throws don't average to a whole number #> ℹ Two or more throws land on the same beat (collision)

title: Get started with jugglr
text: Vanilla siteswap one prop per beat alternating hands. jugglr handles four notation types complex juggling styles.

title: Get started with jugglr
text: hands throw time, written pairs parentheses: x suffix throw value marks crossing side. * shorthand indicates pattern alternates two states — jugglr expands full sequence:
code: siteswap("(4,4)") #> ✔ '(4,4)' is valid synchronous siteswap #> ℹ It uses 4 props #> ℹ It is symmetrical with period 2 siteswap("(4,2x)*") #> ✔ '(4,2x)*' is valid synchronous siteswap #> ℹ Full sequence: (4,2x)(2x,4) #> ℹ It uses 3 props #> ℹ It is symmetrical with period 4

title: Get started with jugglr
text: Multiple props thrown one hand simultaneously, square brackets grouping :
code: siteswap("[43]1") #> ✔ '[43]1' is valid multiplex siteswap #> ℹ It uses 4 props #> ℹ It is asymmetrical with period 2

title: Get started with jugglr
text: hands throwing time, multiplex groups:
code: siteswap("(4,[42x])*") #> ✔ '(4,[42x])*' is valid synchronous multiplex siteswap #> ℹ Full sequence: (4,[42x])([42x],4) #> ℹ It uses 5 props #> ℹ It is symmetrical with period 4

title: Get started with jugglr
text: Patterns multiple jugglers. <|B> notation separates juggler’s sequence; p throw value marks pass juggler: Six props two jugglers, passing every throw. Experienced passers often prefer fractional notation, .5 throw indicates ’s pass:
code: siteswap("<3p 3|3p 3>") #> ✔ '<3p 3|3p 3>' is valid passing siteswap #> ℹ It uses 6 props across 2 jugglers #> ℹ It is asymmetrical with period 2 siteswap("<4.5 3 3 | 3 4 3.5>") #> ✔ '<4.5 3 3 | 3 4 3.5>' is valid passing siteswap #> ℹ It uses 7 props across 2 jugglers #> ℹ It is symmetrical with period 3

title: Get started with jugglr
text: sequence numbers tells much pattern. jugglr provides two diagrams, revealing different aspects sequence. diagrams colour throws prop using colour-blind-friendly Okabe-Ito palette (seven props, ggplot2’s default scale takes ). colours can passed animate() consistency across static animated views — see animation article. timeline() ladder() return ggplot2 objects, can customise standard ggplot2 function:
code: timeline(ss_423) +   ggplot2::labs(title = "My favourite three-ball pattern")

title: Get started with jugglr
text: timeline() draws arcs: arc represents one throw, height proportional throw value, colour-coded prop. ’s roughly pattern looks like side.  Follow single colour track one prop pattern. arc shows thrown, high, lands.
code: timeline(ss_423)

title: Get started with jugglr
text: ladder() shows schedule: beats across axis, lines connecting throws catches. Straight lines self-throws; crossing lines go hand.  timeline shows shape pattern air, ladder shows mechanics hand--hand.
code: ladder(ss_423)

title: Get started with jugglr
text: diagrams become instructive something goes wrong:   collision visible: two arcs land beat, two lines converge point. colours show props appear nowhere need two places . useful debugging tool invented pattern doesn’t feel right — diagram tells exactly breaks.
code: timeline(ss_21) ladder(ss_21)

title: Get started with jugglr
text: diagrams adapt notation type. synchronous patterns throw hands , timeline drawn two-sided: one hand’s arcs sit faint centre line ’s mirror , throw labels.  ladder follows suit, numbering even beats, since hands throw together:
code: ss_sync <- siteswap("(4,2x)*") timeline(ss_sync) ladder(ss_sync)

title: Get started with jugglr
text: hand throws two identical props , arcs otherwise land top . diagrams fan throws apart — slightly different heights timeline, separate curves ladder — prop stays visible:
code: ss_mult <- siteswap("[22]2") timeline(ss_mult) ladder(ss_mult)

title: Get started with jugglr
text: diagrams extend naturally passing patterns, one lane per juggler:   Passes appear arcs (lines) cross juggler lanes.
code: ss_pass <- siteswap("<3p 3|3p 3>") timeline(ss_pass) ladder(ss_pass)

title: Get started with jugglr
text: n_cycles argument controls many repetitions show — default 3 usually enough see full structure, increase trace individual props . ladder() also accepts direction = "vertical" prefer orientation.

title: Get started with jugglr
text: want build visualisation work directly numbers, throw_data() returns underlying data frame: One row per throw: thrown, hand, throw value, lands, prop belongs . data drives diagrams.

title: Get started with jugglr
text: jugglr can also produce animated GIFs patterns via JugglingLab server. ’s 423 animated three colours:  animation article covers full range options: colour modes, prop types, speed controls, save animations disk use documents.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Ella Kaye. Author, maintainer, copyright holder.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Kaye E (2026). jugglr: Juggling Pattern Validation Visualisation. R package version 0.0.0.9000, https://github.com/EllaKaye/jugglr.
code: @Manual{,   title = {jugglr: Juggling Pattern Validation and Visualisation},   author = {Ella Kaye},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://github.com/EllaKaye/jugglr}, }

title: Juggling Pattern Validation and Visualisation
text: jugglr R package validating visualising juggling patterns expressed siteswap notation. siteswap() function factory auto-detects notation type returns S7 object appropriate subclass: vanillaSiteswap, synchronousSiteswap, multiplexSiteswap, synchronousMultiplexSiteswap, passingSiteswap, inherit abstract Siteswap parent class. Functions timeline(), ladder(), throw_data() work across types via dedicated methods subclass.

title: Juggling Pattern Validation and Visualisation
text: can install development version jugglr GitHub : full introduction package, see vignette("jugglr"). full range animation options, see animation article.
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("EllaKaye/jugglr")

title: Juggling Pattern Validation and Visualisation
text: siteswap() function auto-detects notation type returns appropriate subclass. object’s print method reports whether pattern valid, many props requires, period symmetry.

title: Juggling Pattern Validation and Visualisation
code: library(jugglr) ss423 <- siteswap("423") ss423 #> ✔ '423' is valid vanilla siteswap #> ℹ It uses 3 props #> ℹ It is symmetrical with period 3

title: Juggling Pattern Validation and Visualisation
text: Alternation notation also supported: siteswap("(4,2x)*") expands * full two-beat cycle.
code: ss44 <- siteswap("(4,4)") ss44 #> ✔ '(4,4)' is valid synchronous siteswap #> ℹ It uses 4 props #> ℹ It is symmetrical with period 2

title: Juggling Pattern Validation and Visualisation
text: Fractional notation (e.g. "<4.5 3 3 | 3 4 3.5>") also supported passing patterns. Multiplex ("[43]1") synchronous multiplex ("(4,[42x])*") patterns supported .
code: ss_pass <- siteswap("<3p 3|3p 3>") ss_pass #> ✔ '<3p 3|3p 3>' is valid passing siteswap #> ℹ It uses 6 props across 2 jugglers #> ℹ It is asymmetrical with period 2

title: Juggling Pattern Validation and Visualisation
text: Patterns juggled caught construction time:
code: ss21 <- siteswap("21") ss21 #> ✖ '21' is not a valid juggling pattern #> ℹ The throws don't average to a whole number #> ℹ Two or more throws land on the same beat (collision)

title: Juggling Pattern Validation and Visualisation
text: timeline() ladder() work across siteswap types, returning ggplot2 objects can customised. throw_data() returns underlying data frame use custom visualisations.

title: Juggling Pattern Validation and Visualisation
text: plots also useful understanding non-valid sequences jugglable. can see, example, two props need caught time (permissible vanilla siteswap). prop shown different colour, can see balls disappearing needing suddenly appear.
code: timeline(ss423) ladder(ss423) timeline(ss21) ladder(ss21)

title: Juggling Pattern Validation and Visualisation
text: jugglr provides wrapper JugglingLab GIF server. animate() function accepts valid siteswap sequences plain strings Siteswap object. Note JugglingLab GIF server recognise fractional notation, passingSiteswap objects using fractional notation animated. called Positron RStudio, animate() show animation Viewer pane, otherwise browser. path argument supplied, animation saved location instead. Note can take several seconds animation render. save GIF embedding R Markdown Quarto document, pass path argument animate(), reference saved file knitr::include_graphics(). Display options can set chunk arguments:
code: animate("423", path = "man/figures/423-animation.gif", colors = c("#E69F00", "#56B4E9", "#009E73"))

title: Create a siteswap object — siteswap
text: Creates typed siteswap object detecting notation style sequence. Vanilla siteswaps (alphanumeric strings "531") produce vanillaSiteswap object; synchronous siteswaps (,b) notation "(4,2x)*" produce synchronousSiteswap object; multiplex siteswaps square-bracket groups "[43]1" produce multiplexSiteswap object; passing siteswaps <|B> notation "<3p 3 3 3 3 3 | 3p 3 3 3 3 3>" produce passingSiteswap object.

title: Create a siteswap object — siteswap
code: siteswap(sequence)

title: Create a siteswap object — siteswap
text: sequence single character string siteswap notation.

title: Create a siteswap object — siteswap
text: vanillaSiteswap, synchronousSiteswap, multiplexSiteswap, synchronousMultiplexSiteswap, passingSiteswap S7 object.

title: Create a siteswap object — siteswap
code: siteswap("531") #> ✔ '531' is valid vanilla siteswap #> ℹ It uses 3 props #> ℹ It is symmetrical with period 3 siteswap("(4,2x)*") #> ✔ '(4,2x)*' is valid synchronous siteswap #> ℹ Full sequence: (4,2x)(2x,4) #> ℹ It uses 3 props #> ℹ It is symmetrical with period 4 siteswap("[43]1") #> ✔ '[43]1' is valid multiplex siteswap #> ℹ It uses 4 props #> ℹ It is asymmetrical with period 2 siteswap("(2,4)([4x4],2x)") #> ✔ '(2,4)([4x4],2x)' is valid synchronous multiplex siteswap #> ℹ It uses 4 props #> ℹ It is asymmetrical with period 4 siteswap("<3p 3 3 3 3 3 | 3p 3 3 3 3 3>") #> ✔ '<3p 3 3 3 3 3 | 3p 3 3 3 3 3>' is valid passing siteswap #> ℹ It uses 6 props across 2 jugglers #> ℹ It is asymmetrical with period 6

title: Animate a juggling pattern — animate
text: Generates animated GIF juggling pattern via JugglingLab GIF server displays RStudio viewer (default browser). path given GIF saved disk instead.

title: Animate a juggling pattern — animate
code: animate(   pattern,   colors = NULL,   prop = NULL,   bps = NULL,   width = NULL,   height = NULL,   fps = NULL,   slowdown = NULL,   ...,   path = NULL )

title: Animate a juggling pattern — animate
text: pattern siteswap pattern string (e.g. "531") siteswap object: vanillaSiteswap, synchronousSiteswap, multiplexSiteswap, synchronousMultiplexSiteswap, passingSiteswap. Passing patterns p-notation (e.g. "<3p 3|3p 3>") animate correctly; passing patterns fractional notation (e.g. "<4.5 3 3 | 3 4 3.5>") recognised JugglingLab animated. colors Optional. vector R colours (one per prop), one special strings "mixed" "orbits". Passed JugglingLab. prop Prop type: "ball", "ring", "image". NULL (default) JugglingLab default (ball) used. bps Beats per second (numeric scalar). Controls animation speed. width, height Width height animation pixels (numeric scalars). fps Frames per second (numeric scalar). slowdown Slowdown factor (numeric scalar). JugglingLab default 2.0; values greater slow animation , values less 2.0 speed . ... Additional named arguments passed JugglingLab GIF server. Pattern-setting arguments include dwell, hands, body, propdiam, gravity, bouncefrac, squeezebeats, hss, handspec, dwellmax, hold. Animation arguments include stereo, border, camangle, showground, hidejugglers. values must scalars. path Path save GIF (must end .gif). NULL (default) animation displayed viewer.

title: Animate a juggling pattern — animate
text: Invisibly returns path temporary HTML file (displaying) save path (path given).

title: Animate a juggling pattern — animate
text: Note setting colors can introduce short delay server renders animation.

title: Animate a juggling pattern — animate
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ animate("531") animate(vanillaSiteswap("531"), prop = "ring", bps = 5) animate("531", path = tempfile(fileext = ".gif")) } # }

what: jugglr: Juggling Pattern Validation and Visualisation — jugglr-package
title: jugglr: Juggling Pattern Validation and Visualisation — jugglr-package
text: Validate visualise juggling patterns expressed siteswap notation. Supports vanilla, synchronous, multiplex, synchronous multiplex, passing siteswap, tools check pattern validity, produce timeline arc diagrams ladder diagrams, retrieve raw throw data custom visualisations, animate patterns via 'JugglingLab' animation server (https://jugglinglab.org/).

title: jugglr: Juggling Pattern Validation and Visualisation — jugglr-package
text: Maintainer: Ella Kaye ella.kaye@gmail.com [copyright holder] Authors: Ella Kaye ella.kaye@gmail.com [copyright holder]

what: Plot a ladder diagram for a siteswap — ladder
title: Plot a ladder diagram for a siteswap — ladder
text: Produces ladder diagram showing prop paths hands across beats. rails represent hands rungs represent beats. throw connects catch: cross-hand throws drawn straight segments rails, -hand throws curve back rail. Throws coloured prop using colour-blind-friendly Okabe-Ito palette (ggplot2 default scale used beyond seven props), beat numbers labelled along axis.

title: Plot a ladder diagram for a siteswap — ladder
code: ladder(   siteswap,   n_cycles = 3,   direction = c("horizontal", "vertical", "h", "v"),   title = TRUE,   subtitle = TRUE,   ... )

title: Plot a ladder diagram for a siteswap — ladder
text: siteswap vanillaSiteswap, synchronousSiteswap, multiplexSiteswap, synchronousMultiplexSiteswap, passingSiteswap object. n_cycles Number complete cycles simulate (default 3). direction Orientation diagram: "horizontal" (default, time runs left right) "vertical" (time runs top bottom). Shorthands "h" "v" also accepted. title Logical. TRUE (default), adds title showing siteswap sequence. Set FALSE suppress; override ggplot2::labs() returned plot. subtitle Logical. TRUE (default), adds subtitle showing siteswap type number props. Set FALSE suppress; override ggplot2::labs() returned plot. ... Additional arguments passed methods. passingSiteswap objects, hand_gap (default 2) sets vertical spacing juggler's two hands diagram.

title: Plot a ladder diagram for a siteswap — ladder
text: ggplot2 object.

title: Plot a ladder diagram for a siteswap — ladder
text: ladder() S7 generic. methods available following classes: jugglr::multiplexSiteswap jugglr::passingSiteswap jugglr::synchronousMultiplexSiteswap jugglr::synchronousSiteswap jugglr::vanillaSiteswap multiplex throws overlap (e.g. [33]), lines fan apart slightly visible. Passing patterns use one pair rails per juggler, spaced hand_gap throws jugglers cross rails. result standard ggplot2 object, beyond title subtitle toggles can modify adding ggplot2 function, example ggplot2::labs() ggplot2::scale_colour_manual().

title: Plot a ladder diagram for a siteswap — ladder
code: # Vanilla: straight cross-hand throws, curved same-hand throws ladder(siteswap("423"))   # Synchronous: even-only beat numbering, crossing denoted by "x" ladder(siteswap("(4,2x)(2x,4)"))  ladder(siteswap("(2,6x)([6x4x],2x)"))   # Multiplex: identical simultaneous throws fan into separate curves ladder(siteswap("[33]"), n_cycles = 6)   # Passing: one pair of rails per juggler ladder(siteswap("<3p 3|3p 3>"))  ladder(siteswap("<4.5 3 3 | 3 4 3.5>"))   # Vertical orientation, and customise like any ggplot2 object ladder(siteswap("423"), direction = "v", subtitle = FALSE) +   ggplot2::labs(title = "423 or W")

title: Multiplex siteswap — multiplexSiteswap
text: Creates multiplex siteswap object sequence square brackets group simultaneous throws hand. example, "[43]1" means juggler throws heights 4 3 simultaneously first beat, single 1 second beat. slot without brackets single throw exactly vanilla siteswap.

title: Multiplex siteswap — multiplexSiteswap
code: multiplexSiteswap(sequence = character(0))

title: Multiplex siteswap — multiplexSiteswap
text: sequence single character string multiplex siteswap notation, e.g. "[43]1" "[33]".

title: Multiplex siteswap — multiplexSiteswap
text: multiplexSiteswap S7 object.

title: Multiplex siteswap — multiplexSiteswap
text: @type Always "multiplex" (read-). @slots List integer vectors, one element per time slot. Single-throw slots length-1 vectors; multiplex slots length > 1. @throws Integer vector individual throw heights (flattened across slots). @period Number time slots per cycle. @symmetry "symmetrical" period odd; "asymmetrical" period even. @n_props Total throw heights divided period — number props required. (Note: sum(throws) / period, mean(throws).) @can_throw TRUE every slot number props thrown equals number props landing (conservation violation). @satisfies_average_theorem TRUE n_props whole number. @valid TRUE can_throw satisfies_average_theorem TRUE.

title: Multiplex siteswap — multiplexSiteswap
code: multiplexSiteswap("[43]1") #> ✔ '[43]1' is valid multiplex siteswap #> ℹ It uses 4 props #> ℹ It is asymmetrical with period 2 multiplexSiteswap("[33]") #> ✔ '[33]' is valid multiplex siteswap #> ℹ It uses 6 props #> ℹ It is symmetrical with period 1  s <- multiplexSiteswap("[43]1") s@n_props #> [1] 4 s@valid #> [1] TRUE

title: Passing siteswap — passingSiteswap
text: Creates passing siteswap object multi-juggler sequence <|B> notation. section separated | gives one juggler's throws; p suffix (e.g. "3p") marks throw passes next juggler. Fractional notation (e.g. "<4.5 3 3 | 3 4 3.5>") also supported, .5 throws passes second juggler half beat later.

title: Passing siteswap — passingSiteswap
code: passingSiteswap(sequence = character(0))

title: Passing siteswap — passingSiteswap
text: sequence single character string passing siteswap notation, e.g. "<3p 3 3 3 3 3 | 3p 3 3 3 3 3>" "<4.5 3 3 | 3 4 3.5>". Spaces within sections ignored; <3p33|3p33> <3p 3 3|3p 3 3> equivalent.

title: Passing siteswap — passingSiteswap
text: passingSiteswap S7 object.

title: Passing siteswap — passingSiteswap
text: p-notation, jugglers fully synchronised: throw beat alternate hands together. fractional notation, second juggler offset half beat combined timeline.

title: Passing siteswap — passingSiteswap
text: @type Always "passing" (read-). @is_fractional TRUE fractional notation; FALSE p-notation. @n_jugglers Number jugglers (sections sequence). @sequences_by_juggler List character vectors, one per juggler, giving raw tokens (e.g. c("3p", "3", "3")). @throws_by_juggler List numeric vectors, one per juggler, throw heights (integers p-notation; may include .5 fractional). @is_pass_by_juggler List logical vectors, one per juggler, indicating throws passes. @period Number throws per juggler per cycle. @symmetry "symmetrical" period odd; "asymmetrical" period even. @n_props Total number props (sum(throws) / period). @can_throw TRUE two throws land (juggler, beat, hand) triple (collision). @satisfies_average_theorem TRUE n_props whole number. @valid TRUE can_throw satisfies_average_theorem TRUE.

title: Passing siteswap — passingSiteswap
code: passingSiteswap("<3p 3 3 3 3 3 | 3p 3 3 3 3 3>") #> ✔ '<3p 3 3 3 3 3 | 3p 3 3 3 3 3>' is valid passing siteswap #> ℹ It uses 6 props across 2 jugglers #> ℹ It is asymmetrical with period 6 passingSiteswap("<4p 3 | 3 4p>") #> ✔ '<4p 3 | 3 4p>' is valid passing siteswap #> ℹ It uses 7 props across 2 jugglers #> ℹ It is asymmetrical with period 2  # Compact and spaced forms are equivalent s1 <- passingSiteswap("<3p33|3p33>") s2 <- passingSiteswap("<3p 3 3 | 3p 3 3>") identical(s1@throws_by_juggler, s2@throws_by_juggler) #> [1] TRUE  s <- passingSiteswap("<4p 3 | 3 4p>") s@n_props #> [1] 7 s@valid #> [1] TRUE

title: Synchronous multiplex siteswap — synchronousMultiplexSiteswap
text: Creates synchronous multiplex siteswap object. combines synchronous notation (hands throw simultaneously, written pairs parentheses) multiplex notation (square brackets group simultaneous throws hand). example, "(2,4)([4x4],2x)" 4-prop pattern second slot hand 0 throwing two balls simultaneously.

title: Synchronous multiplex siteswap — synchronousMultiplexSiteswap
code: synchronousMultiplexSiteswap(sequence = character(0))

title: Synchronous multiplex siteswap — synchronousMultiplexSiteswap
text: sequence single character string synchronous multiplex siteswap notation, e.g. "(2,4)([4x4],2x)".

title: Synchronous multiplex siteswap — synchronousMultiplexSiteswap
text: synchronousMultiplexSiteswap S7 object.

title: Synchronous multiplex siteswap — synchronousMultiplexSiteswap
text: individual throw heights must even. x suffix marks crossing throw; trailing * indicates pattern alternates two mirrored versions.

title: Synchronous multiplex siteswap — synchronousMultiplexSiteswap
text: @type Always "synchronous multiplex" (read-). @full_sequence expanded sequence * shorthand resolved. @throws Character vector slot throw strings across one expanded cycle. Multiplex groups kept single elements, e.g. "[4x4]". @throws_by_hand Named list elements hand_1 hand_2, character vector throw strings per slot (one per sync slot). @period Number throw slots per full cycle (2 × number sync slots, since hands throw every beat; always even). @symmetry "symmetrical" pattern mirror image; "asymmetrical" otherwise. @n_props Number props: sum throw heights divided period. @can_throw TRUE thrown landing prop counts balance every (slot, hand) pair within one cycle. @satisfies_average_theorem TRUE n_props whole number. @valid TRUE can_throw satisfies_average_theorem TRUE.

title: Synchronous multiplex siteswap — synchronousMultiplexSiteswap
code: synchronousMultiplexSiteswap("(2,4)([4x4],2x)") #> ✔ '(2,4)([4x4],2x)' is valid synchronous multiplex siteswap #> ℹ It uses 4 props #> ℹ It is asymmetrical with period 4 synchronousMultiplexSiteswap("(4,[42x])*") #> ✔ '(4,[42x])*' is valid synchronous multiplex siteswap #> ℹ Full sequence: (4,[42x])([42x],4) #> ℹ It uses 5 props #> ℹ It is symmetrical with period 4  s <- synchronousMultiplexSiteswap("(2,4)([4x4],2x)") s@n_props #> [1] 4 s@valid #> [1] TRUE

title: Synchronous siteswap — synchronousSiteswap
text: Creates synchronous siteswap object two-handed simultaneous-throw sequence. Synchronous notation describes patterns hands throw time, written pairs throw heights parentheses "(4,4)" "(4,2x)*". x suffix marks crossing throw; trailing * indicates pattern alternates two mirrored versions.

title: Synchronous siteswap — synchronousSiteswap
code: synchronousSiteswap(sequence = character(0))

title: Synchronous siteswap — synchronousSiteswap
text: sequence single character string synchronous siteswap notation, e.g. "(4,4)" "(4,2x)*".

title: Synchronous siteswap — synchronousSiteswap
text: synchronousSiteswap S7 object.

title: Synchronous siteswap — synchronousSiteswap
text: @type Always "synchronous" (read-). @full_sequence expanded sequence * shorthand resolved. @throws Character vector individual throw values across one expanded cycle. @throws_by_hand Named list elements hand_1 hand_2, character vector throws hand per slot. @period Number throw slots per full cycle (counts hands per simultaneous beat, always even). @symmetry "symmetrical" pattern mirror image; "asymmetrical" otherwise. @n_props Mean slide sequence, equal number props. @can_throw TRUE collisions occur slide sequence. @satisfies_average_theorem TRUE n_props whole number. @valid TRUE can_throw satisfies_average_theorem TRUE.

title: Synchronous siteswap — synchronousSiteswap
code: synchronousSiteswap("(4,4)") #> ✔ '(4,4)' is valid synchronous siteswap #> ℹ It uses 4 props #> ℹ It is symmetrical with period 2 synchronousSiteswap("(4,2x)*") #> ✔ '(4,2x)*' is valid synchronous siteswap #> ℹ Full sequence: (4,2x)(2x,4) #> ℹ It uses 3 props #> ℹ It is symmetrical with period 4  s <- synchronousSiteswap("(4,2x)*") s@n_props #> [1] 3 s@valid #> [1] TRUE

what: Get throw data for a siteswap — throw_data
title: Get throw data for a siteswap — throw_data
text: Returns data frame describing throw simulated pattern, including beat hand throw originates , lands, prop thrown. underpins timeline() ladder(), can used directly custom visualisations.

title: Get throw data for a siteswap — throw_data
code: throw_data(siteswap, n_cycles = 3, ...)

title: Get throw data for a siteswap — throw_data
text: siteswap siteswap object: vanillaSiteswap, synchronousSiteswap, multiplexSiteswap, synchronousMultiplexSiteswap, passingSiteswap. n_cycles Number complete cycles simulate (default 3). Increase patterns many props ensure appear data. Setting n_cycles >= period * n_props * 2 guarantees prop thrown least twice. ... Additional arguments passed methods.

title: Get throw data for a siteswap — throw_data
text: data frame one row per throw columns: beat: throw beat index hand: throwing hand (0 1) throw: throw height (integer) catch_beat: beat prop lands catch_hand: hand catches (0 1) prop: prop identifier (integer) Synchronous types add is_crossing column. Passing types instead include juggler, is_pass, catch_juggler columns identifying juggler throws catches prop.

title: Get throw data for a siteswap — throw_data

what: Plot a timeline diagram for a siteswap — timeline
title: Plot a timeline diagram for a siteswap — timeline
text: Produces timeline (arc) diagram showing trajectory prop across beats. arc represents throw, height proportional throw value coloured prop using colour-blind-friendly Okabe-Ito palette (ggplot2 default scale used beyond seven props). x-axis labels show throw heights siteswap sequence.

title: Plot a timeline diagram for a siteswap — timeline
code: timeline(siteswap, n_cycles = 3, title = TRUE, subtitle = TRUE, ...)

title: Plot a timeline diagram for a siteswap — timeline
text: siteswap vanillaSiteswap, synchronousSiteswap, multiplexSiteswap, synchronousMultiplexSiteswap, passingSiteswap object. n_cycles Number complete cycles simulate (default 3). warning issued props appear within simulated range. title Logical. TRUE (default), adds title showing siteswap sequence. Set FALSE suppress; override ggplot2::labs() returned plot. subtitle Logical. TRUE (default), adds subtitle showing siteswap type number props. Set FALSE suppress; override ggplot2::labs() returned plot. ... Additional arguments passed methods.

title: Plot a timeline diagram for a siteswap — timeline
text: ggplot2 object.

title: Plot a timeline diagram for a siteswap — timeline
text: timeline() S7 generic. methods available following classes: jugglr::multiplexSiteswap jugglr::passingSiteswap jugglr::synchronousMultiplexSiteswap jugglr::synchronousSiteswap jugglr::vanillaSiteswap See Behaviour siteswap type section details differences resulting plots. result standard ggplot2 object, beyond title subtitle toggles can modify adding ggplot2 function, example ggplot2::labs() ggplot2::scale_colour_manual().

title: Plot a timeline diagram for a siteswap — timeline
text: Vanilla multiplex patterns drawn single-sided, arcs single baseline. x-axis labels throw heights, multiplex labels keep slot brackets (example [54]). Synchronous synchronous multiplex patterns drawn two-sided: one hand's arcs sit faint centre line hand's arcs sit . hand gets x-axis labels, subtitle notes two-sided layout. Multiplex patterns (synchronous ) throw two identical props beat fan arcs slightly different peak heights, otherwise-overlapping throws render concentric parabolas. Passing patterns drawn one lane per juggler; passes arc lanes. Fractional notation offsets beat positions accordingly.

title: Plot a timeline diagram for a siteswap — timeline
code: # Vanilla: single-sided arcs timeline(siteswap("423"))   # Synchronous: two-sided, hands mirrored about a centre line timeline(siteswap("(4,2x)(2x,4)"))  timeline(siteswap("(2,6x)([6x4x],2x)"))   # Multiplex: identical simultaneous throws fan to distinct heights timeline(siteswap("[54]24"))  timeline(siteswap("[33]"))   # Passing: one lane per juggler, with passes arcing between them timeline(siteswap("<3p 3|3p 3>"))  timeline(siteswap("<4.5 3 3 | 3 4 3.5>"))   # Increasing n_cycles helpful for sequences with short period timeline(siteswap("3"), n_cycles = 6)   # ggplot2 object: customise like any other plot timeline(siteswap("423")) + ggplot2::labs(title = "423 or W")

title: Vanilla siteswap — vanillaSiteswap
text: Creates vanilla siteswap object alphanumeric siteswap sequence "531" "97531". Vanilla siteswap describes solo juggling patterns one prop thrown per beat, alternating hands.

title: Vanilla siteswap — vanillaSiteswap
code: vanillaSiteswap(sequence = character(0))

title: Vanilla siteswap — vanillaSiteswap
text: sequence single character string vanilla siteswap notation (digits letters , e.g. "531").

title: Vanilla siteswap — vanillaSiteswap
text: vanillaSiteswap S7 object.

title: Vanilla siteswap — vanillaSiteswap
text: @type Always "vanilla" (read-). @throws Integer vector throw heights one cycle. @period Number throws per cycle (length throws). @symmetry "symmetrical" period odd (pattern repeats swapped hands); "asymmetrical" period even. @n_props Mean throw height, equal number props required. @can_throw TRUE two throws land beat (collisions). @satisfies_average_theorem TRUE n_props whole number. @valid TRUE can_throw satisfies_average_theorem TRUE.

title: Vanilla siteswap — vanillaSiteswap
code: vanillaSiteswap("531") #> ✔ '531' is valid vanilla siteswap #> ℹ It uses 3 props #> ℹ It is symmetrical with period 3 vanillaSiteswap("97531") #> ✔ '97531' is valid vanilla siteswap #> ℹ It uses 5 props #> ℹ It is symmetrical with period 5  s <- vanillaSiteswap("531") s@n_props #> [1] 3 s@valid #> [1] TRUE

title: jugglr (development version)
text: First release. jugglr validates visualises juggling patterns written siteswap notation. siteswap() detects notation style sequence returns typed S7 object: vanillaSiteswap(), synchronousSiteswap(), multiplexSiteswap(), synchronousMultiplexSiteswap(), passingSiteswap(). constructor also exported direct use. Passing siteswap supports p-notation fractional notation. Printing siteswap object reports whether pattern valid, , valid patterns, number props, period, symmetry. Invalid patterns report (average theorem collisions). timeline() draws arc diagram ladder() draws ladder diagram, every siteswap type returning ggplot2 objects. throw_data() returns underlying throw--throw data frame custom visualisations. animate() produces animated GIF pattern via JugglingLab animation server, options colours, prop type, speed.
